notes:
đấy is a sentence-ending particle. It adds emphasis like “you know,” “I’m telling you,” or “just so you know.”

It usually goes at the end of a statement to stress or remind the listener of something. It can show mild insistence, warning, or reassurance, depending on tone.  

Very close to đó — in the South, people prefer đó in everyday speech, while đấy is more Northern. Tone of voice matters a lot: soft tone = gentle reminder; stronger tone = warning or correction.
notes_md:
**đấy** is a sentence-ending particle. It adds emphasis like “you know,” “I’m telling you,” or “just so you know.”

It usually goes at the end of a statement to stress or remind the listener of something. It can show mild insistence, warning, or reassurance, depending on tone.  


Very close to **đó** — in the South, people prefer *đó* in everyday speech, while *đấy* is more Northern. Tone of voice matters a lot: soft tone = gentle reminder; stronger tone = warning or correction.
exampleSentencesTranslated: He drinks hot tea, you know.
She buys a new shirt, you know.

definitions.en: A sentence-ending particle used for emphasis or as a demonstrative.
definitions.es: Una partícula demostrativa utilizada para enfatizar o indicar algo.
definitions.vi: Một trợ từ cuối câu dùng để nhấn mạnh hoặc chỉ định.

sentenceBreakdowns: Em (I) + ăn (eat) + cơm tối (dinner) + đấy (you know)
Anh (he) + uống (drink) + trà nóng (hot tea) + đấy (you know)
Chị (she) + mua (buy) + áo mới (new shirt) + đấy (you know)
